title: Gassy Genesis Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Gassy Genesis Evangelion?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:52 am Here's something that's bugged me for a while and I don't recall seeing a thread on it. We know that roughly translated, Shin Seiki means "New Century/Era", and Seiki links to the Biblical Genesis (Reichu can help me out with this). So, in an attempt to translate it to the full Greek by Gainax it became "Neon Genesis", roughly changing it to "New Beginning/Origin". HOWEVER, there is one glaring mistake in that the Greek for "New" is "Neos", not "NEON". Neon is "a chemically inert gaseous element occurring in small amounts in the earth's atmosphere, used chiefly in a type of electrical lamp." You can read about it HERE , but in particular it seems the -on suffix is "a suffix used in the names of subatomic particles (gluon; meson; neutron), quanta (graviton), and other minimal entities or components." Was this accidental or intentional?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:56 am Gassy seems more appropriate since Anno is full of hot air.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:56 am I read somewhere that the creators just wanted the title to sound cool. There may be some connection to meanings, but I think that is what they ultimately wanted from the start, for it to sound cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:56 am Anno doesn't know shit about Greek language so there

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:59 am Action_Bastard wrote: There may be some connection to meanings, but I think that is what they ultimately wanted from the start, for it to sound cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 11:01 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Action_Bastard wrote: There may be some connection to meanings, but I think that is what they ultimately wanted from the start, for it to sound cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 25, 2008 11:09 am Xard wrote: No one suggested "change"

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jul 25, 2008 12:04 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: You can read about it HERE ,

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Jul 25, 2008 12:35 pm This topic is inert gas.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 25, 2008 1:33 pm Zuggy wrote: From link wrote: from Greek. neon, neutral gender form of neos meaning "new,"

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 25, 2008 2:05 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Zuggy wrote: From link wrote: from Greek. neon, neutral gender form of neos meaning "new,"

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 2:12 pm Genesis was obviously translated to Creation by him.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Fri Jul 25, 2008 2:17 pm Action_Bastard wrote: Genesis was obviously translated to Creation by him.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 2:19 pm Sure, have you heard of evangelical churches, they teach the gospel, so.... Correct me if I am wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jul 25, 2008 4:04 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: but how is neos, uhhh, not gender neutral?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 25, 2008 5:02 pm Zuggy wrote: What do you mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 5:26 pm Wow, I feel good now!

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Fri Jul 25, 2008 5:27 pm This reminds me, lately I've been wondering what would happen if an evangelion tried farting.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 5:29 pm lol, funny, but also try asking that here and see what you get. [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=5332&highlight=[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Da Games Elite » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:27 pm From what I've read, certain languages have numerous ways of saying the exact same phrase. I never studyed Greece, but I know a lot of Romantic languages, like Italian and Latin, which Italian is derived from, have a lot of synonyms. I think Neos and Neon are just synonyms of each other. So maybe they had an idea what they were talking about. Just maybe.
